channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Blank to blank
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WPLN listener Nancy Bieschke and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WPLN listener Nancy Bieschke and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: A little boy loses his orange 'Balloon' but gains a new friend in this kids' book
item.description: When author Bruce Handy's son was young, he loved and lost an orange balloon. He and illustrator Julie Kwon talk about a child's singular devotion to a lost object in their nearly wordless kids' book.
item.content:encoded: When author Bruce Handy's son was young, he loved and lost an orange balloon. He and illustrator Julie Kwon talk about a child's singular devotion to a lost object in their nearly wordless kids' book.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Balaban

item.title: World Press Photo Contest winners cast a lens on resilience, pain and bliss
item.description: A selection of prize honorees from the 2026 World Press Photo Contest capture the pain of the past year — but also focus on moments of strength, determination and joy.
item.content:encoded:
A selection of prize honorees from the 2026 World Press Photo Contest capture the pain of the past year — but also focus on moments of strength, determination and joy.

(Image credit: Ihsaan Haffejee for GroundUp)
item.dc:creator: Kamala Thiagarajan

item.title: Worldwide Pinhole Photography Day
item.description: Fine art photographer Nancy Breslin joins NPR's Scott Simon in studio to celebrate Worldwide Pinhole Photography Day.
item.content:encoded:
Fine art photographer Nancy Breslin joins NPR's Scott Simon in studio to celebrate Worldwide Pinhole Photography Day.

(Image credit: Nancy Breslin)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: New museum dedicated to AI promises an ethical approach
item.description: As some continue to pushback on AI art, artist Refik Anadol seeks to base his new venture on ethical and environmental principles.
item.content:encoded:
Machine Dreams: Rainforest, the inaugural exhibition at Dataland. The museum dedicated to AI art is scheduled to open on June 20.'/>

As some continue to pushback on AI art, artist Refik Anadol seeks to base his new venture on ethical and environmental principles.

(Image credit: Refik Anadol/Dataland)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: "The Midnight Rebellion" is a choose-your-own-adventure podcast
item.description: WBUR's "The Midnight Rebellion" is a choose-your-own-adventure podcast. Co-creator Dean Russell tells NPR's Rob Schmitz how that works, and why it's a good way for kids to explore the climate crisis.
item.content:encoded: WBUR's "The Midnight Rebellion" is a choose-your-own-adventure podcast. Co-creator Dean Russell tells NPR's Rob Schmitz how that works, and why it's a good way for kids to explore the climate crisis.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Rocky Balboa statue takes up a new home inside Philly art museum
item.description: The bronze sculpture is on display inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art as part of a new exhibition on the impact and cultural importance of statues.
item.content:encoded:
The bronze sculpture is on display inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art as part of a new exhibition on the impact and cultural importance of statues.

(Image credit: Tassanee Vejpongsa)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for April 25, 2026: With Not My Job guest Father James Martin
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with guest host Tom Papa, special guest Father James Martin and panelists Josh Gondelman, Shantira Jackson , and Katie Nolan
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with guest host Tom Papa, special guest Father James Martin and panelists Josh Gondelman, Shantira Jackson  , and Katie Nolan

(Image credit: Dia Dipasupil)

item.title: Before sci-fi was everywhere, this pioneering magazine championed 'scientifiction'
item.description: The name didn't stick. The fan communities did.
item.content:encoded:
The name didn't stick. The fan communities did.

(Image credit: Amazing Stories)
item.dc:creator: Chris Klimek

item.title: If you feel like Shakespeare isn't for you, look deeper
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Adjoa Andoh, the inaugural Director's Resident at the Folger Shakespeare Library, about Shakespeare's relevance in modern times, and specifically to people of color.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Adjoa Andoh, the inaugural Director's Resident at the Folger Shakespeare Library, about Shakespeare's relevance in modern times, and specifically to people of color.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers
